description: Root extension point, inherited by every stack. Universal, low-footprint plugins: up-to-date library docs (Context7) and active security review (Security Guidance). Semgrep + Serena are declared but OPT-IN (not auto-enabled) to avoid always-on bloat.

plugins.description: Context7 (Upstash) - injects up-to-date, version-specific library docs and real code examples into the prompt, killing hallucinated/stale APIs. Stack-agnostic (useful in every direction), small footprint. FREE, works with no account; an optional CONTEXT7_API_KEY only raises rate limits. NOTE: the docs index is a hosted Upstash service, so it is not a true offline/local tool. Complements the language servers (LSP = your repo's symbols; Context7 = external canonical docs).

Security Guidance (Anthropic, official) - ACTIVE runtime hooks, not prose: PostToolUse regex warnings on every Edit/Write (~25 dangerous patterns), a Stop-hook LLM diff review, and an agentic cross-file review on git commit (injection/XSS/SSRF/IDOR/auth-bypass/unsafe-deserialization/secrets). Fully local (uses your Claude session, no external calls). COMPLEMENTARY to rules/security.md (that guidance shapes what gets written BEFORE; this catches unsafe code AFTER + at commit). NOTE: the Stop-hook review is an extra LLM call per turn (cost/latency); its commit review overlaps in place - not in purpose - with this config's secrets-gate.mjs.

VERIFIED 2026-07: this is 'Semgrep Guardian' (source: github.com/semgrep/mcp-marketplace), NOT a local-only wrapper around the OSS semgrep CLI - it is a closed-source hook binary + MCP server ('guardian') that registers on SessionStart (async), PreToolUse/PostToolUse (Write|Edit|Bash), and UserPromptSubmit. Its own README's install steps end with 'ask claude to login to semgrep, using the guardian mcp' - a Semgrep AppSec Platform (cloud) login is part of normal setup, opening a browser auth flow (observed: fires again on every terminal/session restart via the SessionStart hook). No documented local/offline mode exists in its manifest. OPT-IN (not auto-enabled) - if you want a no-cloud-login setup, do NOT enable this; use rules/security.md + security-guidance@claude-plugins-official instead (both fully local). Only enable if cloud-connected guardrails + the login are acceptable.

Serena - semantic, LSP-based code analysis and refactoring across the repo (symbol-level edits, find references). Fully local, no account. OPT-IN (not auto-enabled): partially overlaps the per-language LSP plugins already enabled per stack, so enable it only when you want the heavier semantic-editing layer.
